herb_constituents.name: saponins
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga saponins are bioactive compounds known for their potential therapeutic benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These saponins have been studied for their ability to exert cardiovascular protective effects and may also have antispasmodic and anti-cancer activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the mechanisms and applications of ammi visnaga saponins in medicinal uses.

herb_constituents.name: flavonoids
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga flavonoids have been found to possess various medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. These flavonoids have been studied for their potential in treating cardiovascular diseases, such as hypertension and atherosclerosis. Furthermore, they may also have neuroprotective effects, potentially contributing to the prevention of neurodegenerative diseases.

herb_constituents.name: tannins
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga tannins are a group of polyphenolic compounds known for their antioxidant properties and potential health benefits. They have been studied for their ability to exert anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, contributing to their use in traditional medicine. The tannins present in ammi visnaga may also have a role in modulating cardiovascular health and reducing oxidative stress.

herb_constituents.name: sesquiterpenes
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga sesquiterpenes have been identified as the key active constituents responsible for the medicinal properties of this plant. These sesquiterpenes have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to the plant's therapeutic effects. The sesquiterpenes in ammi visnaga have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.

herb_constituents.name: coumarins
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga coumarins, particularly scopoletin, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to their potential therapeutic effects. Scopoletin has also been reported to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, highlighting its broad range of bioactivity. Additionally, scopoletin has been studied for its potential to inhibit platelet aggregation, which may be beneficial in the management of cardiovascular disorders.

herb_constituents.name: alkaloids
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga alkaloids, particularly lasiocarpine, have been studied for their potential medicinal properties. Research suggests that these alkaloids may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could contribute to their therapeutic benefits. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the mechanisms and potential applications of ammi visnaga alkaloids in medicine.

herb_constituents.name: glycosides
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga glycosides, particularly sesquilabdane-type furanoflavonoids, exhibit potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These compounds have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic benefits and potential applications of ammi visnaga glycosides.

herb_constituents.name: phenolic acids
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga phenolic acids have been found to possess various bioactive properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. These phenolic compounds may contribute to the plant's traditional use in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and mechanisms of action of ammi visnaga phenolic acids.

herb_constituents.name: quinones
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga quinones are a group of bioactive compounds found in the plant Ammi visnaga, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The quinones present in Ammi visnaga have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Ammi visnaga quinones in treating various medical conditions.

herb_constituents.name: terpenoids
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga terpenoids, which are a class of medicinal compounds found in the plant, have been shown to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Research suggests that these terpenoids may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Additionally, some studies have investigated the potential of ammi visnaga terpenoids for their neuroprotective effects.

herb_constituents.name: furanocoumarins
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga furanocoumarins, also known as seselin and isofuraticin, are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to have potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can contribute to the plant's use in traditional medicine. Research has also suggested that seselin may have a role in lowering blood pressure due to its vasodilatory properties.

herb_constituents.name: aromatic acids
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga aromatic acids, also known as asaronic acid, are a group of aromatic compounds found in the plant Ammi visnaga. They have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antispasmodic and diuretic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The aromatic acids in Ammi visnaga have been studied for their potential therapeutic benefits, including their ability to relax smooth muscle and increase urine production.

herb_constituents.name: xanthones
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga xanthones are known to possess significant medicinal properties, particularly in relation to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. These compounds have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which may contribute to various diseases. The xanthones found in ammi visnaga have shown promise in the development of new therapeutic agents for treating various conditions.

herb_constituents.name: betulinic acid
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga betulinic acid is a medicinal active constituent derived from the plant Ammi visnaga, also known as khella. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension and heart conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and applications in modern medicine.

herb_constituents.name: bupleurone
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga bupleurone, a sesquiterpene lactone, is a bioactive compound isolated from the plant Ammi visnaga, which has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Research suggests that bupleurone exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of various conditions. Further studies are needed to fully elucidate the mechanisms and therapeutic applications of bupleurone.

herb_constituents.name: isoalantolactone
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga isoalantolactone is a sesquiterpene lactone known for its potential anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, which have been the subject of various scientific studies. Research suggests that isoalantolactone may have inhibitory effects on certain enzymes and pathways, contributing to its therapeutic potential. Further investigation is needed to fully understand the mechanisms and applications of isoalantolactone derived from Ammi visnaga.

herb_constituents.name: dehydroisoalantolactone
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga dehydroisoalantolactone is a medicinal active constituent known for its spasmolytic and diuretic properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including edema and hypertension, due to its ability to relax smooth muscles and promote urine production. Research has also explored its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

herb_constituents.name: alantolactone
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga alantolactone is a sesquiterpenoid lactone found in Ammi visnaga, a plant used in traditional medicine. It has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including respiratory issues and as a diuretic. Research has also identified its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

herb_constituents.name: bupleurin
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga bupleurin, a medicinal active constituent, is known for its potential anti-inflammatory properties. It has also been studied for its effects on the cardiovascular system, including its ability to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure. Bupleurin's mechanism of action is thought to involve the inhibition of certain enzymes involved in inflammation and blood vessel constriction.

herb_constituents.name: bupleroside
herb_constituents.desc: Ammi visnaga bupleroside is a medicinal active constituent known for its cardiotonic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular conditions such as hypertension. It is believed to work by stimulating the heart and increasing its contractility, thereby improving blood circulation and reducing blood pressure. Research on bupleroside has also suggested its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
